comments.commentText: Im confused on the second solution. Can someone explain?

answers: Try every possible subarray and compute its sum.

Idea is to keep track of each sum in a HashMap.
If: currentPrefixSum = k
Then: previous PrefixSum = currentPrefixSum - k
So while iterating:
Keep track of prefix sum.
Store the first occurrence of each prefix sum in a HashMap.
If (prefixSum - k) exists in the map -> we found a valid subarray.
Why store the first occurrence?
Because we want the longest subarray.
description: Find the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum equals k.
title: Longest Subarray with Sum K

notes: A path can start and end at any node
Use DFS and track max path sum

description: Given a binary tree, find the maximum path sum. A path is any sequence of nodes connected by edges, and it can start and end at any node in the tree.
title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
constraints: Path must contain at least one node
